page: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with 32 virtual CPUs utilizing a shared allocation model, mapped from 16 physical cores on an AMD EPYC processor running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is limited to 1 GB of network-attached storage, and the instance does not include a GPU. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance falls into the average tier due to the shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is a primary strength of this instance; large-block memory write bandwidth and PassMark memory scores rank in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded operations also perform exceptionally well, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks reaching top-tier status.

Given its hardware profile and performance characteristics, this server is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core CPU utilization. Appropriate use cases include static web serving, Redis key-value databases, and text generation or prompt processing for small-to-medium language models. The shared CPU architecture offers a cost-efficient alternative for resource-dense workloads where memory capacity and bandwidth are prioritized over dedicated compute cores.
description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network-attached local storage. It operates without a GPU. The instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier memory write bandwidth, though its shared CPU allocation places multi-core performance in the average tier. It is highly efficient for single-threaded compression and memory-bound tasks, making it suitable for static web serving, Redis deployments, and small-scale language model inference.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server combining thirty-two shared AMD EPYC vCPUs with high-bandwidth memory for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory
Top-tier memory write bandwidth and PassMark memory performance
1 GB of network-attached local storage with no integrated GPU
Strong single-core CPU execution and high-speed single-threaded compression
Well-suited for static web serving, Redis databases, and small LLM inference
